zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Robert Booth於1382年出生在英國英格蘭藍卡爾巴頓時，他的父親John Boothe of Barton, Esq.29歲，母親Lady Joanna Trafford24歲。他於1409年5月3日與Douce Venables在英國藍卡爾埃克爾斯結婚。他們至少是11個兒子和9個女兒的父母。他去世於1460年9月14日在英國爾鄧翰 梅西享年78歲，埋葬在英國爾維爾姆斯洛福。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Robert Booth于1382年出生在英国英格兰藍卡爾巴頓时，他的父亲John Boothe of Barton, Esq.29岁，母亲Lady Joanna Trafford24岁。他于1409年5月3日与Douce Venables在英国藍卡爾埃克爾斯结婚。他们至少是11个儿子和9个女儿的父母。他去世于1460年9月14日在英国爾鄧翰 梅西享年78岁，埋葬在英国爾維爾姆斯洛福。

nameMeanings.name: Booth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (northern): topographic or occupational name from Middle English bothe (Old Danish bōth) ‘temporary shelter, such as a covered market stall or a cattle-herdsman's hut’. The latter sense was predominant in the Pennines of Lancashire and Yorkshire, where there were many cattle farms or vaccaries, and whose subdivisions were known as ‘booths’. The principal meaning of the surname there was therefore probably ‘cattle herdsman’, ‘man in charge of a vaccary’, and thus identical with Boothman . Elsewhere it may have denoted a shopkeeper who owned a temporary market stall, but no evidence has been found to confirm this use of the surname. In the British Isles the surname is still more common in northern England, where Scandinavian influence was more marked, and in Scotland, where the word was borrowed into Gaelic as both(an).
History: Robert Booth (1604–72) is mentioned in the colonial records of Exeter, NH, in 1645. He subsequently moved to ME.

variants.name: Boothman
meanings: English (northern): occupational name from Middle English both (Old Danish bōth) ‘temporary shelter, such as a covered market stall or a cattle-herdsman's hut’ + man. In the Pennine uplands of northern England, where booths were subdivisons of vaccaries or cattle-rearing establishment, the name probably denoted a herdsman who looked after cattle, perhaps a man in charge of a vaccary. Elsewhere the surname might have denoted a shopkeeper but evidence for this lacking. Alternatively, the name can also be an occupational name for a servant of a man surnamed Booth .

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Robert Booth was born in 1382, in Barton, Lancashire, England, United Kingdom, his father, John Boothe of Barton, Esq., was 29 and his mother, Lady Joanna Trafford, was 24. He married Douce Venables on 3 May 1409, in Eccles, Lancashire, England. They were the parents of at least 11 sons and 9 daughters. He died on 14 September 1460, in Dunham Massey, Cheshire, England, at the age of 78, and was buried in Wilmslow, Cheshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Robert Booth est né en 1382, à Barton, Lancashire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John Boothe of Barton, Esq., avait 29 ans et sa mère, Lady Joanna Trafford, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Douce Venables le 3 mai 1409, à Eccles, Lancashire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 11 fils et 9 filles. Il est décédé le 14 septembre 1460, à Dunham Massey, Cheshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 78 ans, et a été enterré à Wilmslow, Cheshire, Angleterre.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Robert Boothが1382年にイギリスイギリスランカシュアイアバートンで生まれた時，父John Boothe of Barton, Esq.は29歳，母Lady Joanna Traffordは24歳であった。1409年5月3日にイギリスランカシュアイアエククレスでDouce Venablesと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも11人の息子と9人の娘の親であった。彼は1460年9月14日にイギリスチェシュアイアダンハム マセイで78歳で死去し，イギリスチェシュアイアウィルムスロウにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Robert Booth(이)가 1382년 영국 잉글랜드 랭카실르 바턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Boothe of Barton, Esq.(은)는 29세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Joanna Trafford(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1409년 5월 3일 영국 랭카실르 c클스에서 Douce Venables(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 열 명 이상의 아들과 아홉 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1460년 9월 14일 영국 체실르 던함 마시에서 78세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 체실르 윌슬로에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Robert Booth nasceu em 1382, em Barton, Lancashire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, John Boothe of Barton, Esq., tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Lady Joanna Trafford, tinha 24 anos. Ele casou-se com Douce Venables em 3 de maio de 1409, em Eccles, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 11 filhos e 9 filhas. Ele faleceu em 14 de setembro de 1460, em Dunham Massey, Cheshire, Inglaterra, com 78 anos, e foi sepultado em Wilmslow, Cheshire, Inglaterra.

ro.Dunham, Chester, England: Dunham Massey, Cheshire, Anglia, United Kingdom

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Robert Booth родился в 1382 г. в местности бартон, ланкашир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени John Boothe of Barton, Esq. было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Lady Joanna Trafford, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Douce Venables 3 мая 1409 г. в местности экклесь, ланкашир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 11 сыновей и 9 дочерей. Он умер 14 сентября 1460 г. в местности дунам масси, чешир, Англия в возрасте 78 лет и был похоронен в местности уилмслоу, чешир, Англия.

Se casó con Douce Venables el 3 de mayo de 1409, en Eccles, Lancashire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 11 hijos y 9 hijas. Murió el 14 de septiembre de 1460, en Dunham Massey, Cheshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 78 años, y fue sepultado en Wilmslow, Cheshire, Inglaterra.
